Items.Title: 更新！ 你的简历是什么？ | Updated! What's your bio?
Items.Description:
所以大约 4 年，我发现自己需要为在会议上发言而写传记。 我联系了dev.to寻求想法。

从那以后，我又在几次会议上发表了讲话，并且发现自己一直在调整那个生物。 就像谈话描述一样，总感觉还有改进的余地。 你改变。 你的谈话改变了。 为什么不确保这反映在您的 BIOS 中？

所以这是我开始使用的示例格式（取自对 dev.to 的评论）

他/她拥有 {#} 年的 {Technology Name} 经验，专注于 {Technology Focus}。 在他/她的业余时间，{Name} 喜欢 {Personal Interest #1} 和 {Personal Interest #2}。

瑞安

这确实让我得到了我的第一个简历：

她拥有 12 年的 Java 软件工程师经验，专注于 API 和微服务，并于今年 5 月涉足 Ruby。 在业余时间，她是一个狂热的长篇阅读者，热爱所有真实犯罪的播客，并正在尝试完成主要的阿斯汤加瑜伽系列。

现在我的简历看起来有点像这样：

她专业地帮助解决 WP Engine 身份团队的问题。 她喜欢与 Ruby 合作，向人们展示长期的 Git 提交令人印象深刻，并帮助编写安全、易于阅读的软件来支持她公司的授权和身份验证。

从那时起有什么变化？ 嗯，2018年我所在的公司被收购了。 我已经在团队中移动了 3-4 次，并且参加了更多的会议。

对我来说，你想从简历中得到的是，这个人是谁，我为什么要听他们的？ 看到那个人的个性也不会错。 我喜欢奇思妙想和积极向上，并且有很多其他的兴趣与我为什么要为我的职业生涯无关。 我的事业不是我的生活。 我希望我的简历能证明这一点。

所以让我介绍一个更新的会议简历模板：

在 {company} 任教、编码、设计、架构师、构建…} {tech stack}，对{技术/专业焦点}充满热情，并已在该领域工作了 {#} 年。 我谈论的是 MFA，所以我提到这就是我的职业生涯。}

你怎么看？ 这是否让您了解您想了解的有关会议发言人的信息？

原文:

So about 4 years, I found myself in need of writing a biography for speaking at a conference. I reached out to dev.to for ideas.

Since then, I have spoken at a couple more conference and have found myself tweaking that bio along the way. Just like talk descriptions, it always feels like there is room for improvement. You change. Your talk changes. Why shouldn’t you make sure that is reflected in your bios?

So here was a sample format that I started with(taken from a comment on dev.to)

{Name} is from {City, State} where he/she works as an {Title} at {Company}. He/She has {#} years of experience with {Technology Name} with a focus on {Technology Focus}. In his/her spare time, {Name} enjoys {Personal Interest #1} and {Personal Interest #2}.

Ryan

And that did get me one of my first bios:

Christine is from Omaha, Nebraska where she works as a full stack Ruby on Rails engineer at Flywheel, WordPress hosting for creatives. She has 12 years experience as a Java software engineer with a focus on APIs and micro-services and took the plunge into Ruby just this May. In her spare time she is an avid long form reader, lover of all true-crime podcasts and is attempting to work through the primary Ashtanga yoga series.

Now my bio looks a bit like this:

Christine is a lifetime learner from Omaha, NE (in the middle of the USA), where she likes to read too much and eats food that probably took too long to prepare. Professionally she’s helping solve problems on the Identity team at WP Engine. She loves working with Ruby, showing people that long Git commits are impressive, and helping write secure, easy-to-read software that powers authorization and authentication at her company.

What changed from that time? Well, the company I was at in 2018 was acquired. I have moved around teams 3-4 times and been to many more conferences.

To me, what you want to get out of a bio is, who is this person, and why should I listen to them? And it wouldn’t be wrong to see a bit of that person’s personality. I love whimsy and positivity and have tons of other interests that have nothing to do with why I do it for my career. My career is not my life. I want my bio to show that.

So let me introduce an updated conference bio template:

{Name} is from {Current Location} who enjoys {Personal Passion 1}, {Personal Passion 2}, and {Personal Passion 3} . {They/She/Him} professionally {verb ex. teach, code, design, architect, build…} {tech stack} at {company} and are passionate about {Technology/Professional focus} and have been involved in the field for {#} years. {Last sentence tie it back to your talk experience, ex. I talk about MFA, so I mention that is what I am involved with my career.}

Christine’s 2022 Technical Conference Speaker BIO Template

What do you think? Does this get you what you want to know about your conference speakers?

Kathryn Grayson Nanz - Aug 30

Items.Title: 我如何从每小时 9 美元到以 7 位数的价格出售公司（第 1 部分） | How I Went From $9/hr To Selling A Company For 7 Figures (Part 1)
Items.Description:
我是一名大学辍学生，从 Team Treehouse 和 CodeAcademy 等网站自学编码。 去年，我运行了不到 4 年的 SAAS 初创公司以 7 位数的价格卖掉了它。

这篇文章是关于如何从学习编码开始的！

在文职工作中，我感到非常不满足和没有得到充分利用，所以我很高兴有几个月的时间来寻找新的东西。

我被解雇的那天，我叔叔让我帮他建立一个网站（他有一个本地服务业务），我真的很喜欢这个挑战。 我建立了一个基本的 Wordpress 网站，编写并优化了几个页面，很高兴看到它在短短几周内直接为他带来了更多的业务和电话。

我在谷歌搜索时发现了一个编码课程，以学习如何使我叔叔的标志更大：D

真正让我兴奋的是来自 Team Treehouse 的关于如何构建您的第一个网站的精彩课程，通过该课程，我能够非常快速地从现有设计完全靠自己构建一个漂亮的网站。

很难描述在如此短的时间内看到如此出色的结果的感觉。

多年前，我参加过一门关于计算机编程的大学课程，但几乎没能通过。 一些更抽象的编码工作对我虚弱的大脑来说太过分了……但这是我真正喜欢的东西。

大约在同一时间，我发现了一个名为 Elance（现在称为 UpWork）的网站，我很高兴看到人们免费获得数百或数千美元的报酬来做我刚刚为我叔叔所做的事情。

我开始申请工作并很快得到了我的第一份工作，以 777 美元的价格构建了 7 个自定义 WordPress 主题。 如果我现在看到那份工作清单，我会认为有人为获得那么少的报酬而疯狂，但显然工作描述对我来说足够合理，我并不害怕“支付我的教育费用”。

在决定我可能需要寻找工作之前，我一直从事自由职业并从事本地网页设计工作大约一年半。 自由职业是一次很棒的学习经历，但我不是一个出色的营销人员，而且勉强勉强过活。 报税季快到了，我没办法存钱，所以我有麻烦了……

因此，我没有寻找固定价格的演出，而是开始通过同一个市场以及在线其他一些地方寻找小时工。

我本来可以在当地找工作，但老实说，我不知道我能胜任什么职位……

大约一个半月后，我看到了一份非常适合我技能的工作清单——尽管可能有点牵强。 第二天我申请并通过Skype接受了面试。

在我们的电话中，企业主问了我几个关于我的技能和能力的问题，然后说“你被录用了！每小时 35 美元怎么样？”

我非常震惊，因为这几乎是我 4 年来上一个小时费率（9 美元 / 小时）的 4 倍，但我从下周一开始工作，我的薪水和我的技能一起从那里增加了！

最终，这些相同的技能让我能够做我真正喜欢的事情，制作 6 位数，构建我自己的销售 7 位数的 SAAS 产品，并通过教其他人做同样的事情来赚钱。

所以……这就是我的故事。 你怎么看？ 问我一个问题！



原文:

I'm a college dropout who taught myself to code from websites like Team Treehouse and CodeAcademy. Last year I sold my SAAS startup for 7 figures after running it for just under 4 years.

This post is about how it all started with learning to code!

In 2013 I was laid off from my $9/hr job and had a few months of severance pay to hold me over. I had felt really unfulfilled and underutilized in a clerical job, so I was excited to have a few months to figure something new out.

The day I got laid off my uncle asked me to help him put up a website (he had a local service business) and I really enjoyed the challenge. I put up a basic Wordpress website, wrote and SEO optimized a few pages, and was happy to see that it directly led to more business and phone calls for him within just a few weeks.

I discovered a coding course while googling to learn how to make my uncle's logo bigger :D

The big thing that really lit me up was a wonderful course from Team Treehouse on How to Build Your First Website, through which I was able to very quickly build a beautiful website from an existing design completely on my own.

It's hard to describe how it felt to see such great results in such a short amount of time.

I had taken a college course on computer programming many years prior, but had almost failed the course. Some of the more abstract coding work was too much for my feeble brain... but here was something that I really enjoyed.

Around the same time I discovered a website called Elance (now called UpWork) and was thrilled to see that people were getting paid hundreds or thousands of dollars to do what I had just done for my uncle for free.

I started applying for jobs and quickly got my first job to build 7 custom WordPress themes for $777. If I saw that job listing now I would think someone was crazy for getting paid that little, but apparently the job description was just reasonable enough for me to apply and I wasn't afraid to "pay for my education".

I kept picking up freelance work and doing local web design jobs for about a year and a half before deciding I might need to look for a JOB. Freelancing was a great learning experience but I wasn't a great marketer and was barely scraping by. Tax season was around the corner and I hadn't been able to save anything so I was going to be in trouble...

So instead of looking for fixed price gigs, I started looking for hourly work through the same marketplace as well as some other places online.

I could have looked at jobs locally, but to be honest I didn't know what job titles I would be qualified for...

After about a month and a half I saw a job listing that was perfect for my skillset - though maybe a bit of a stretch. I applied and got an interview over Skype the next day.

On our call the business owner asked me a few questions about my skills and abilities, then said "You're hired! How's $35/hr?"

I was pretty shell shocked since that was almost 4 times my last hourly rate ($9/hr) which I was at for 4 years, but I started work the next Monday and my salary just increased from there along with my skills!

Eventually those same skills allowed me to make 6 figures doing something I really enjoy, build my own SAAS product which sold for 7 figures and make money teaching other people to do the same.

So... that's my story. What do ya think? Ask me a question!
